# Task Log - Add UI Type to Cartridge JSON Schema

**Date:** December 20, 2025  
**Time:** 15:43  
**Version Target:** 0.0.2

---

## Task Overview

Enhance the cartridge JSON schema to include UI type information for TextGame cartridges. This will enable Ubiquitous to determine which UI implementation to use when loading a game cartridge.

### Task Checklist

- [ ] Analyze current cartridge JSON structure
- [ ] Design UI type field for JSON schema
- [ ] Update cartridge JSON examples with UI type field
- [ ] Update cartridge template with UI type field
- [ ] Update Game.cs to read UI type field
- [ ] Update CartridgeListWindow to display UI type
- [ ] Update documentation to reflect new schema
- [ ] Test loading cartridges with UI type field
- [ ] Verify backward compatibility

---

## Progress Log

### [15:43] Session Started
Beginning implementation of UI type field in cartridge JSON schema.

### Analysis Phase

**Current Cartridge Structure:**
- Title (string)
- Author (string)
- Version (string)
- Description (string)
- StartingRoomId (string)
- Rooms (array)
- Items (array)

**UI Type Options for TextGame:**
1. "TextBased" - Traditional console-style text interface
2. "Graphical" - Enhanced visual interface with graphics
3. "Hybrid" - Combination of text and graphical elements

**Proposed Schema Addition:**
```json
{
  "Title": "Game Title",
  "Author": "Author Name",
  "Version": "1.0.0",
  "Description": "Game description",
  "UIType": "TextBased",
  "StartingRoomId": "room_id",
  ...
}
```

**Default Behavior:**
- If UIType field is not present, default to "TextBased" for backward compatibility
- Field should be optional but recommended for new cartridges

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Update JSON Schema
1. Add UIType field to cartridge JSON files
2. Update all example cartridges
3. Update template cartridge

### Phase 2: Update Game Loading
1. Modify Game.cs to parse UIType field
2. Add UIType property to Game class
3. Implement default value handling

### Phase 3: Update UI Components
1. Update CartridgeListWindow to show UI type
2. Add UI type indicator in cartridge selection
3. Update any relevant UI components

### Phase 4: Documentation
1. Update API documentation
2. Update cartridge creation guide
3. Update CHANGELOG.md

### Phase 5: Testing
1. Test loading cartridges with UIType field
2. Test loading legacy cartridges without UIType field
3. Verify UI type is correctly displayed
4. Confirm backward compatibility

## Notes

- This feature sets the foundation for supporting multiple UI implementations in the future
- The UIType field should be extensible to support additional game types beyond TextGame
- Consider adding validation to ensure only valid UI types are accepted
- Future versions may want to add UI-specific configuration options
